steps.title: Identify the structures separated by the blank
steps.content: The independent clause ends at “civil rights,” and what follows is a noun phrase—”the former establishing a Latino rights advocacy group and the latter legally affirming the rights of Latino students”—that functions as an appositive, clarifying what those events represented.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
A comma introduces a non-essential appositive phrase. Choice C correctly places a comma after “history,” separating the main clause from the appositive phrases that follow.

The other choices either use incorrect punctuation (A, D) or introduce an extraneous word that breaks the appositive structure (B).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
